news_summary: Advancements in graphene superconductors, innovative remote-controlled bio-robots, ambitious US quantum computing goals, and enhanced privacy features on WhatsApp highlight today's exciting technology progress.
news_summary_fr: Les avancées dans le domaine des supraconducteurs à base de graphène, les bio-robots innovants télécommandés, les objectifs ambitieux des États-Unis en matière d'informatique quantique et les fonctionnalités renforcées de protection de la vie privée sur WhatsApp illustrent les progrès technologiques passionnants de notre époque.
news_summary_es: Los avances en los superconductores de grafeno, los innovadores biorrobots controlados a distancia, los ambiciosos objetivos de Estados Unidos en materia de computación cuántica y las mejoras en las funciones de privacidad de WhatsApp ponen de relieve los emocionantes avances tecnológicos de hoy.

articles.title: Family of magnetic field-boosted superconductors in rhombohedral graphene
articles.summary: Nature, Published online: 29 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10815-xFamily of magnetic field-boosted superconductors in rhombohedral graphene
articles.content: In some unconventional superconductors, time-reversal symmetry can be broken in addition to the gauge symmetry1, resulting in superconductivities that can be enhanced or induced by magnetic fields2. However, field-enhanced superconductors are more vulnerable to impurities than Bardeen-Cooper-Schrieffer counterparts3. Crystalline rhombohedral multilayer graphene is a promising platform to explore them due to its superior material quality and gate-tunable strong correlation effects4,5. Here we report transport measurements of rhombohedral tetralayer and pentalayer graphene, demonstrating a spectrum of clean-limit superconductivities. We found three different types of field-enhanced and field-induced superconductivities in the pentalayer. They are all robust against an in-plane field up to 8.5 Tesla, exceeding the Pauli limit by tens of times. Compared to Bernal bilayer graphene showing only in-plane field-enhancement6, pentalayer graphene features superconductors enhanced by out-of-plane as well as in-plane fields. They also reside at much lower gate electric fields owing to the intrinsically flatter band dispersion—facilitating their study and further engineering. Additionally, we observed that proximitized spin-orbit coupling (SOC) generates multiple new superconductors without introducing additional disorder effects. Our work establishes a new family of magnetic field-boosted superconductors in rhombohedral graphene. Utilizing the high accessibility with moderate gate voltages, this will pave the way for realizing non-Abelian quasiparticles through interfacial engineering7 in the extreme clean limit, in that proximitized SOC leads to topological states8 and maintains the ultrahigh quality of crystalline graphene.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant scientific breakthrough in superconductivity using rhombohedral graphene, demonstrating new types of magnetic field-enhanced superconductors with robust properties and potential for topological quantum states. This advancement has broad implications for future quantum technologies and materials science, representing a meaningful positive impact beyond a niche audience.
articles.personality_title: New types of superconductors discovered in rhombohedral graphene boosted by magnetic fields
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Scientists study materials called superconductors that can carry electricity without losing energy. Usually, magnetic fields make superconductors weaker, but some special types get stronger when a magnetic field is applied. Graphene, a very thin and strong material made of carbon, is a good place to explore these unusual superconductors.

**What happened** – Researchers tested layers of rhombohedral graphene, which means the carbon atoms are stacked in a special pattern. They looked at four-layer and five-layer graphene and found three new kinds of superconductors that become stronger when exposed to magnetic fields. These superconductors worked well even with strong magnetic fields up to 8.5 Tesla, much stronger than in similar materials. They also used a technique called spin-orbit coupling to create even more superconductors without making the material dirty or damaged.

**Impact** – This discovery is important because these superconductors are very clean and stable, and they can be controlled with small electric voltages. This makes it easier to study them and use them in future technology. The new superconductors could help scientists build advanced quantum devices, which rely on special particles called non-Abelian quasiparticles, useful for powerful and secure computers.

**What's next step** – Scientists will work on using these superconductors to create and control topological quantum states. These states are promising for developing new quantum technologies. The high quality of the graphene and the ability to tune the superconductors with electric fields make this a strong foundation for future experiments and applications.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Researchers discovered new magnetic field-boosted superconductors in rhombohedral graphene that are stable, easy to control, and could lead to advances in quantum technology.
articles.personality_title_fr: Nouveaux types de supraconducteurs dans le graphène rhomboédrique renforcés par des champs magnétiques
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les scientifiques étudient des matériaux appelés supraconducteurs qui peuvent transporter l’électricité sans perte d’énergie. Habituellement, les champs magnétiques affaiblissent les supraconducteurs, mais certains types spéciaux deviennent plus forts sous un champ magnétique. Le graphène, un matériau très fin et solide composé de carbone, est un bon terrain pour explorer ces supraconducteurs inhabituels.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Des chercheurs ont testé des couches de graphène rhomboédrique, où les atomes de carbone sont empilés d’une manière particulière. Ils ont étudié des graphènes à quatre et cinq couches et ont découvert trois nouveaux types de supraconducteurs renforcés par des champs magnétiques. Ces supraconducteurs fonctionnaient bien même sous des champs magnétiques très forts jusqu’à 8,5 Tesla, beaucoup plus que dans des matériaux similaires. Ils ont aussi utilisé un procédé appelé couplage spin-orbite pour créer encore plus de supraconducteurs sans abîmer le matériau.

**Impact** – Cette découverte est importante car ces supraconducteurs sont très purs et stables, et peuvent être contrôlés avec de faibles tensions électriques. Cela facilite leur étude et leur utilisation dans de futures technologies. Ces nouveaux supraconducteurs pourraient aider à construire des dispositifs quantiques avancés, qui utilisent des particules spéciales appelées quasiparticules non abéliennes, utiles pour des ordinateurs puissants et sécurisés.

**Prochaine étape** – Les scientifiques vont travailler à utiliser ces supraconducteurs pour créer et contrôler des états quantiques topologiques. Ces états sont prometteurs pour le développement de nouvelles technologies quantiques. La grande qualité du graphène et la possibilité de régler les supraconducteurs avec des champs électriques offrent une base solide pour de futures expériences et applications.

**Phrase clé** – Des chercheurs ont découvert de nouveaux supraconducteurs renforcés par des champs magnétiques dans le graphène rhomboédrique, stables, faciles à contrôler, et pouvant ouvrir la voie à des avancées en technologie quantique.
articles.personality_title_es: Nuevos tipos de superconductores en grafeno romboédrico potenciados por campos magnéticos
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Los científicos estudian materiales llamados superconductores que pueden transportar electricidad sin perder energía. Normalmente, los campos magnéticos debilitan a los superconductores, pero algunos tipos especiales se vuelven más fuertes cuando se aplica un campo magnético. El grafeno, un material muy delgado y fuerte hecho de carbono, es un buen lugar para explorar estos superconductores inusuales.

**Qué pasó** – Los investigadores probaron capas de grafeno romboédrico, donde los átomos de carbono están apilados en un patrón especial. Estudiaron grafeno de cuatro y cinco capas y encontraron tres nuevos tipos de superconductores que se fortalecen con campos magnéticos. Estos superconductores funcionaron bien incluso con campos magnéticos fuertes de hasta 8.5 Tesla, mucho más que en materiales similares. También usaron una técnica llamada acoplamiento espín-órbita para crear más superconductores sin dañar el material.

**Impacto** – Este descubrimiento es importante porque estos superconductores son muy limpios y estables, y se pueden controlar con pequeños voltajes eléctricos. Esto facilita su estudio y uso en tecnología futura. Los nuevos superconductores podrían ayudar a construir dispositivos cuánticos avanzados, que usan partículas especiales llamadas cuasipartículas no abelianas, útiles para computadoras potentes y seguras.

**Siguiente paso** – Los científicos trabajarán en usar estos superconductores para crear y controlar estados cuánticos topológicos. Estos estados son prometedores para desarrollar nuevas tecnologías cuánticas. La alta calidad del grafeno y la capacidad para ajustar los superconductores con campos eléctricos hacen de esto una base fuerte para futuros experimentos y aplicaciones.

**Frase clave** – Investigadores descubrieron nuevos superconductores potenciados por campos magnéticos en grafeno romboédrico que son estables, fáciles de controlar y podrían impulsar avances en tecnología cuántica.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of layered, translucent crystalline sheets arranged in a rhombohedral pattern, glowing softly with intertwined golden and blue magnetic field lines weaving through and around the layers, symbolizing enhanced superconductivity; delicate electric currents ripple gently across the surfaces, while subtle abstract waves hint at spin-orbit coupling effects, all rendered in natural, muted earth and sky colors.

articles.title: Remote-controlled cockroach swarm can now breathe underwater
articles.summary: Tiny 3D-printed diving suits allow cockroaches to walk underwater for up to 3 hours with no ill effects, which could enable a cyborg insect swarm to explore disaster zones and perhaps even Mars
articles.content:
Tiny 3D-printed diving suits allow cockroaches to walk underwater for up to 3 hours with no ill effects, which could enable a cyborg insect swarm to explore disaster zones and perhaps even Mars

A cyborg cockroach with a suit for breathing underwater NTU Singapore

Swarms of cyborg insects controlled remotely via electrical implants can now operate underwater, thanks to tiny diving suits supplying them with oxygen – which could one day enable them to explore Mars.

Hirotaka Sato at Nanyang Technological University in Singapore and his colleagues first demonstrated in 2021 that Madagascar hissing cockroaches (Gromphadorhina portentosa) could be remotely controlled with electrodes embedded in sensory organs known as cerci. In 2024, they demonstrated that a swarm of 20 of these cyborg insects could coordinate.

The aim was to develop biological robots equipped with infrared sensors that could be released in large numbers after natural disasters to search for survivors. Cockroaches represent a ready-made platform for such applications with a working fuel source, efficient locomotion and in-built reflexes to dodge obstacles – capabilities that engineers still struggle to replicate mechanically at such a small scale.

But Sato and his team were unhappy with the insects’ inability to search flooded areas, which aren’t uncommon in disaster zones, so they have developed a diving suit to allow them to operate underwater.

Cockroaches breathe through pores called spiracles on their abdomen and thorax. The researchers 3D printed a watertight resin suit, which protects the abdominal spiracles from water. Tiny hoses run forwards from the suit to connect directly to the thorax spiracles; the main part of the suit would interfere with leg movement if it covered the thorax as well.

Free newsletter Sign up to The Daily The latest on what’s new in science and why it matters each day. Sign up to newsletter

Rather than supplying the insects with a pressurised tank of oxygen, as scuba suits do, the researchers included a mixture of hydrogen peroxide and manganese dioxide. When these two chemicals react, the hydrogen peroxide decomposes to produce oxygen, which the cockroach can absorb.

While wearing the suit, the cockroaches were able to walk underwater for up to 3 hours at a time, at depths of up to 50 centimetres, with no ill effects: all five insects that were monitored after wearing the suits were still healthy three days later.

The suits also allowed the insects to move underwater surprisingly naturally. On land, the suit-wearing cyborg insects achieved an average forward speed of 87.5 millimetres per second, and this only slowed to 78.4 millimetres per second underwater.

Sato says such suits could make search-and-rescue cyborg insects far more capable, but he also hopes to explore their use in space, another environment lacking in vital oxygen.

“The ultimate goal is to [take this technology to] space,” he says. “It’s kind of one step, one big step, towards space suits for cyborg insects. Exploration over the Mars surface, for example.”

To this end, the research team now intends to test the cockroach suits in the various harsh conditions that they could encounter in orbit or on the surface of a planet like Mars: very low and high temperatures, a vacuum and intense radiation exposure. However, space agencies may not like the idea of sending cockroaches to Mars because it would risk contaminating the planet with microbes from Earth.

Alan Winfield at the University of the West of England says the concept of scuba-diving cockroaches may seem strange, but it has obvious applications, such as environmental monitoring.

“There have been attempts to build very small robots, but the problem is batteries. With a very small robot, you typically don’t get very much battery life,” he says. “People often used to say to me, what are the three big problems in mobile robots? And I’d say: energy, energy and energy.”

Cockroaches are not only vastly more efficient than robots and able to operate for longer without refuelling, they are also capable of foraging for their own food in the wild and operating almost indefinitely.

Journal reference: Nature Communications DOI: 10.1038/s41467-026-74235-1
articles.reasoning: The article describes a significant technological breakthrough in creating 3D-printed diving suits for cyborg cockroaches, enabling them to operate underwater for extended periods. This innovation has broad potential applications in disaster search-and-rescue operations and space exploration, representing a meaningful advancement with tangible societal and scientific benefits. The story is focused, detailed, and highlights a novel bio-robotic technology with real-world impact.
articles.personality_title: Scientists create diving suits for cyborg cockroaches to explore underwater and beyond
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Scientists have been working on controlling cockroaches with tiny electrical implants to create cyborg insects. These insects could help find survivors after disasters because they move well and avoid obstacles better than small robots.

**What happened** – Researchers at Nanyang Technological University in Singapore made tiny 3D-printed diving suits for Madagascar hissing cockroaches. These suits let the cockroaches breathe underwater for up to 3 hours by producing oxygen from special chemicals inside the suit. The cockroaches could walk normally underwater, and they stayed healthy after wearing the suits.

**Impact** – This is the first time insects controlled like robots can work underwater for so long. It means these cyborg insects could search flooded disaster areas where normal robots cannot go. The suits could also help explore places like Mars, where there is no air to breathe.

**What's next step** – The team plans to test the suits in tough conditions like extreme temperatures, vacuum, and radiation found in space or on Mars. They want to see if the cyborg insects can survive and work in these harsh environments.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Tiny 3D-printed suits now let cyborg cockroaches breathe and move underwater, opening new possibilities for disaster rescue and space exploration.
articles.personality_title_fr: Des combinaisons de plongée pour cafards cyborgs leur permettent d'explorer sous l'eau et au-delà
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Des scientifiques travaillent à contrôler des cafards avec de petits implants électriques pour créer des insectes cyborgs. Ces insectes pourraient aider à trouver des survivants après des catastrophes grâce à leur capacité à se déplacer et éviter les obstacles mieux que les petits robots.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Des chercheurs de l'Université technologique de Nanyang à Singapour ont fabriqué de minuscules combinaisons de plongée imprimées en 3D pour des cafards hissing de Madagascar. Ces combinaisons permettent aux cafards de respirer sous l'eau jusqu'à 3 heures en produisant de l'oxygène grâce à des produits chimiques à l'intérieur. Les cafards pouvaient marcher normalement sous l'eau et sont restés en bonne santé après avoir porté les combinaisons.

**Impact** – C'est la première fois que des insectes contrôlés comme des robots peuvent travailler sous l'eau aussi longtemps. Cela signifie que ces insectes cyborgs pourraient explorer des zones inondées où les robots normaux ne peuvent pas aller. Les combinaisons pourraient aussi aider à explorer des endroits comme Mars, où il n'y a pas d'air à respirer.

**Prochaine étape** – L'équipe prévoit de tester les combinaisons dans des conditions difficiles comme des températures extrêmes, le vide et les radiations présents dans l'espace ou sur Mars. Ils veulent vérifier si les insectes cyborgs peuvent survivre et travailler dans ces environnements.

**Résumé en une phrase** – De minuscules combinaisons imprimées en 3D permettent désormais aux cafards cyborgs de respirer et de se déplacer sous l'eau, ouvrant de nouvelles possibilités pour le sauvetage et l'exploration spatiale.
articles.personality_title_es: Trajes de buceo para cucarachas cyborgs les permiten explorar bajo el agua y más allá
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Científicos han estado controlando cucarachas con pequeños implantes eléctricos para crear insectos cyborgs. Estos insectos podrían ayudar a encontrar sobrevivientes tras desastres porque se mueven bien y evitan obstáculos mejor que los robots pequeños.

**Qué pasó** – Investigadores de la Universidad Tecnológica de Nanyang en Singapur hicieron trajes de buceo diminutos impresos en 3D para cucarachas Madagascar. Estos trajes permiten que las cucarachas respiren bajo el agua hasta por 3 horas al producir oxígeno con químicos especiales dentro del traje. Las cucarachas podían caminar normalmente bajo el agua y permanecieron saludables después de usar los trajes.

**Impacto** – Es la primera vez que insectos controlados como robots pueden trabajar tanto tiempo bajo el agua. Esto significa que estos insectos cyborgs podrían buscar en zonas inundadas donde los robots normales no pueden entrar. Los trajes también podrían ayudar a explorar lugares como Marte, donde no hay aire para respirar.

**Próximo paso** – El equipo planea probar los trajes en condiciones difíciles como temperaturas extremas, vacío y radiación que se encuentran en el espacio o en Marte. Quieren ver si los insectos cyborgs pueden sobrevivir y trabajar en esos ambientes.

**Resumen en una frase** – Pequeños trajes impresos en 3D ahora permiten que cucarachas cyborgs respiren y se muevan bajo el agua, abriendo nuevas posibilidades para rescates y exploración espacial.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of a small swarm of cockroaches wearing delicate, translucent 3D-printed diving suits with tiny oxygen tubes, gracefully walking underwater among softly glowing bubbles and gentle ripples, symbolizing their innovative ability to explore flooded and alien environments.

articles.title: US government wants to have a useful quantum computer by 2028
articles.summary: The US government is trying to speed up the development of quantum computers so it can have one sooner
articles.content:
The US government is trying to speed up the development of quantum computers so it can have one sooner

A central part of a quantum computer built by the company Infleqtion Infleqtion

The US government wants to get hold of a quantum computer good enough to contribute to scientific breakthroughs in just two years. It will use it to try to accelerate the research and development of new materials, pharmaceuticals and molecules useful in agriculture and manufacturing.

Once a dream of theoretical physicists, quantum computers are now undoubtedly real, but have yet to prove unambiguously useful or to have broad commercial value. Their computational power depends on their size – how many components called qubits they comprise – and how reliable they are. Existing devices are still too small and too error-prone.

The US Department of Energy’s Quantum Genesis initiative is targeting 2028 for when this will change. The initiative is launching a competition and plans to build a national quantum supercomputing facility. It is part of the larger Genesis Mission, which is also giving researchers money.

By 2028, the DoE wants quantum computers to be powerful enough to start contributing to open problems in chemistry, materials science, plasma physics and high-energy physics. “I have a lot of confidence that the building blocks exist… we don’t need a massive breakthrough,” says Darío Gil, under secretary for science at the DoE.

Gil says that his confidence is based on recent progress across quantum computing, from how well individual qubits can be built to what he qualifies as phenomenal progress in algorithms that quantum computers use to catch and correct their own errors. Additionally, he thinks that using AI will play a role in reaching the 2028 milestone, for instance, by helping researchers optimise how quantum computers are controlled.

“2028 is quite ambitious but not impossible,” says Juliette Peyronnet at the quantum computing company Alice & Bob. Paul Stimers at the Quantum Industry Coalition says that several quantum computing firms previously announced plans to deliver a scientifically useful, error-proof quantum computer by 2028 or within a few years of that mark.

This announcement from the DoE comes on the heels of President Donald Trump signing two executive orders related to quantum technology, as well as a $2 billion investment into several quantum computing companies from the US Department of Commerce.

Some quantum technologies named in the executive orders, such as quantum sensors, have already reached commercial viability, so it is fairly certain that they will soon be deployed more broadly, for instance, in space through collaboration with NASA. Quantum computing has garnered more attention in part because quantum computers could become powerful encryption-breaking machines, but they may also face comparatively bigger challenges on the road to 2028.

Gil acknowledges that the leap from existing devices to quantum computers that will need to be hundreds or thousands of times larger will require lots of learning. “The reality of it will be dealing with complexity [increase] from [building] a device to a chip to a system,” he says. Additionally, issues may arise with supply chains, which are still fragile because of how exotic many of the components needed for novel quantum devices are, says Stimers.

The US isn’t alone in moving to support and accelerate quantum technology development. The UK, for instance, aims to acquire large-scale quantum computers beyond 2030 through the ProQure program. Quantum computing is also one of the two technologies, alongside artificial intelligence, at the centre of China’s next five-year national development strategy. Notably, targeting 2028 makes for the shortest timeline among the three. “It’s aggressive,” says Gil.
articles.reasoning: The article reports on the US government's ambitious initiative to accelerate the development of useful quantum computers by 2028, which promises significant advancements in scientific research and practical applications across multiple fields such as chemistry, materials science, and pharmaceuticals. This effort has broad societal implications and is supported by substantial funding and a national strategy, indicating meaningful potential benefits beyond niche interests.
articles.personality_title: US aims to build a useful quantum computer by 2028
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Quantum computers use special parts called qubits to solve problems much faster than regular computers. They could help scientists discover new medicines, materials, and technologies. Right now, these computers exist but are still small and make many errors.

**What happened** – The US Department of Energy (DoE) announced a plan called Quantum Genesis to have a powerful, reliable quantum computer by 2028. They will hold a competition and build a national facility to support this goal. The government is also investing money and working with companies to speed up progress.

**Impact** – If successful, this quantum computer could solve important scientific problems in chemistry, physics, and materials science that current computers cannot handle. It would help create new products and technologies faster, benefiting fields like agriculture, manufacturing, and medicine. This effort is faster than similar plans in other countries.

**What's next step** – The DoE will fund researchers and companies, improve quantum parts, and use artificial intelligence to control the computers better. They will also build the infrastructure needed to handle more complex quantum systems.

**One-sentence takeaway** – The US government is pushing to create a powerful quantum computer by 2028 to speed up important scientific discoveries and technology development.
articles.personality_title_fr: Les États-Unis visent à construire un ordinateur quantique utile d'ici 2028
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les ordinateurs quantiques utilisent des éléments spéciaux appelés qubits pour résoudre des problèmes beaucoup plus rapidement que les ordinateurs classiques. Ils pourraient aider les scientifiques à découvrir de nouveaux médicaments, matériaux et technologies. Pour l'instant, ces ordinateurs existent mais sont encore petits et font beaucoup d'erreurs.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Le Département de l'Énergie des États-Unis (DoE) a annoncé un plan appelé Quantum Genesis pour obtenir un ordinateur quantique puissant et fiable d'ici 2028. Ils organiseront un concours et construiront une installation nationale pour soutenir cet objectif. Le gouvernement investit également de l'argent et collabore avec des entreprises pour accélérer les progrès.

**Impact** – Si ce projet réussit, cet ordinateur quantique pourra résoudre des problèmes scientifiques importants en chimie, physique et science des matériaux que les ordinateurs actuels ne peuvent pas gérer. Cela aidera à créer plus rapidement de nouveaux produits et technologies, au bénéfice de secteurs comme l'agriculture, la fabrication et la médecine. Cette initiative est plus rapide que des plans similaires dans d'autres pays.

**Prochaine étape** – Le DoE financera les chercheurs et entreprises, améliorera les composants quantiques et utilisera l'intelligence artificielle pour mieux contrôler les ordinateurs. Il construira aussi l'infrastructure nécessaire pour gérer des systèmes quantiques plus complexes.

**Une phrase clé** – Le gouvernement américain accélère la création d'un ordinateur quantique puissant d'ici 2028 pour favoriser les découvertes scientifiques et le développement technologique.
articles.personality_title_es: Estados Unidos apunta a construir una computadora cuántica útil para 2028
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Las computadoras cuánticas usan partes especiales llamadas qubits para resolver problemas mucho más rápido que las computadoras normales. Podrían ayudar a los científicos a descubrir nuevos medicamentos, materiales y tecnologías. Por ahora, estas computadoras existen pero son pequeñas y cometen muchos errores.

**Qué pasó** – El Departamento de Energía de Estados Unidos (DoE) anunció un plan llamado Quantum Genesis para tener una computadora cuántica poderosa y confiable para 2028. Organizarán una competencia y construirán una instalación nacional para apoyar esta meta. El gobierno también está invirtiendo dinero y trabajando con empresas para acelerar el progreso.

**Impacto** – Si tiene éxito, esta computadora cuántica podrá resolver problemas científicos importantes en química, física y ciencia de materiales que las computadoras actuales no pueden manejar. Ayudará a crear productos y tecnologías más rápido, beneficiando a la agricultura, manufactura y medicina. Este esfuerzo es más rápido que planes similares en otros países.

**Próximo paso** – El DoE financiará a investigadores y empresas, mejorará las partes cuánticas y usará inteligencia artificial para controlar mejor las computadoras. También construirá la infraestructura necesaria para manejar sistemas cuánticos más complejos.

**Una frase clave** – El gobierno de EE.UU. impulsa la creación de una computadora cuántica poderosa para 2028 para acelerar importantes descubrimientos científicos y el desarrollo tecnológico.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed painting of a glowing, intricate quantum chip made of interconnected qubits floating above an open book of scientific research, with soft warm light illuminating the scene, surrounded by abstract, gentle waveforms symbolizing quantum algorithms and AI, all rendered in natural, muted earth tones and soft blues.

articles.title: WhatsApp to let people chat without swapping phone numbers
articles.summary: The app's new usernames feature will be rolled out globally over the next few months.
articles.content:
WhatsApp is set to let people chat without having to reveal their phone number - by exchanging unique usernames instead.

It will be rolled out globally to the platform's three billion account holders over the next few months, the platform said.

From Monday, users will start being able to reserve a name via the app, although it will not be compulsory.

The firm said people would be able to remove or change their usernames at any time.

Once it is fully activated, WhatsApp users will be able to connect after exchanging usernames only. There will still be options to block or report unwanted messages.

Names will be limited to 35 characters and there will be few restrictions, with the exception of some high profile officials and celebrities whose names will not be made available to anyone else.

So it's unlikely WhatsApp will be overrun with users calling themselves Donald Trump, for example.

The Meta-owned firm described usernames as a privacy feature.

Alice Newton-Rex, WhatsApp's head of product, said she had heard from users that they didn't always want to share their phone numbers in order to be in contact with others, particularly in group chats.

She said she hoped the feature would "give users control over how they choose to show up" on the app.
articles.reasoning: WhatsApp's new username feature enhances user privacy by allowing people to chat without sharing phone numbers, benefiting its three billion users globally. This is a significant privacy improvement with broad impact and clear details provided.
articles.personality_title: WhatsApp lets users chat using usernames instead of phone numbers
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – WhatsApp is a popular messaging app used by about three billion people worldwide. Until now, users had to share their phone numbers to chat with others.

**What happened** – WhatsApp announced a new feature that allows users to create unique usernames. Starting soon, people can share these usernames to chat without giving out their phone numbers. Users can reserve a username through the app and can change or remove it anytime. Some famous names will be protected so others cannot use them.

**Impact** – This change helps protect users’ privacy by letting them control who sees their phone number. It is especially useful in group chats or when meeting new people. Users can still block or report unwanted messages, keeping the app safe.

**What's next step** – Over the next few months, WhatsApp will roll out this feature to all its users around the world. People will be able to decide if they want to use usernames instead of phone numbers.

**One-sentence takeaway** – WhatsApp’s new usernames let users connect more privately by chatting without sharing their phone numbers.
articles.personality_title_fr: WhatsApp permet de discuter avec des noms d’utilisateur au lieu de numéros de téléphone
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – WhatsApp est une application de messagerie utilisée par environ trois milliards de personnes dans le monde. Jusqu’à présent, les utilisateurs devaient partager leur numéro de téléphone pour discuter.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – WhatsApp a annoncé une nouvelle fonctionnalité permettant de créer des noms d’utilisateur uniques. Bientôt, les gens pourront partager ces noms pour discuter sans donner leur numéro de téléphone. Les utilisateurs peuvent réserver un nom via l’application et le modifier ou le supprimer à tout moment. Certains noms célèbres seront protégés pour éviter qu’ils soient utilisés par d’autres.

**Impact** – Ce changement protège mieux la vie privée des utilisateurs en leur donnant le contrôle sur qui voit leur numéro. C’est particulièrement utile dans les discussions de groupe ou avec de nouvelles personnes. Les utilisateurs peuvent toujours bloquer ou signaler les messages indésirables, ce qui assure la sécurité.

**Prochaine étape** – Dans les prochains mois, WhatsApp déploiera cette fonctionnalité pour tous ses utilisateurs dans le monde. Chacun pourra choisir d’utiliser un nom d’utilisateur au lieu d’un numéro.

**Résumé en une phrase** – Les nouveaux noms d’utilisateur de WhatsApp permettent de discuter plus en privé sans partager son numéro de téléphone.
articles.personality_title_es: WhatsApp permite chatear usando nombres de usuario en lugar de números de teléfono
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – WhatsApp es una aplicación de mensajería usada por cerca de tres mil millones de personas en todo el mundo. Hasta ahora, para chatear era necesario compartir el número de teléfono.

**Qué pasó** – WhatsApp anunció una nueva función que permite crear nombres de usuario únicos. Pronto, las personas podrán compartir estos nombres para chatear sin dar su número de teléfono. Los usuarios podrán reservar un nombre a través de la aplicación y cambiarlo o eliminarlo cuando quieran. Algunos nombres famosos estarán protegidos para que nadie más pueda usarlos.

**Impacto** – Este cambio mejora la privacidad de los usuarios al permitirles controlar quién ve su número de teléfono. Es especialmente útil en chats grupales o al conocer gente nueva. Los usuarios todavía podrán bloquear o reportar mensajes no deseados para mantener la seguridad.

**Próximo paso** – En los próximos meses, WhatsApp implementará esta función para todos sus usuarios en todo el mundo. Cada persona podrá decidir si quiere usar un nombre de usuario en lugar de un número.

**Resumen en una frase** – Los nuevos nombres de usuario de WhatsApp permiten conectar de forma más privada sin compartir el número de teléfono.
articles.image_prompt: A warm, detailed painting of two glowing, intertwined chat bubbles made of soft, natural-colored threads, connected by a delicate, luminous username tag floating between them, set against a calm, neutral background symbolizing private and secure communication without revealing phone numbers.
